title: dipsacus pinnatifidus
intro: Dipsacus pinnatifidus, also known as Chinese foxglove or Dahuang, is a plant species belonging to the Dipsacaceae family. It is a type of flowering plant used in traditional Chinese medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, such as edema, fever, and digestive issues, due to its ability to reduce swelling and promote urination. Dipsacus pinnatifidus is typically prepared as a decoction, tea, or capsule for its medicinal use, and can also be found in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine formulas.
